name: persona-development-saas
prompt: I need to build buyer personas for our B2B SaaS project management tool. We're targeting mid-market companies (200-1000 employees). Can you help me develop 2-3 detailed personas?

expectations.description: Output contains at least 2 distinct personas with unique names
Each persona includes demographics: role/job title, company size, and industry
Each persona includes psychographics: goals, pain points, and decision-making factors
Each persona includes buying behavior section with trigger, research method, and decision factors
Personas are specific to mid-market B2B SaaS context, not generic templates with placeholder brackets
Output does NOT contain unfilled placeholder brackets like [Job title] or [Size, industry]

name: jtbd-framework-ecommerce
prompt: We run an e-commerce analytics platform and want to understand our customers through Jobs-to-be-Done. Help me create JTBD statements for our key user segments.

expectations.description: Output contains multiple JTBD statements in the 'When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [expected outcome]' format
Output distinguishes between functional jobs and emotional jobs
JTBD statements are specific to e-commerce analytics, referencing concepts like conversions, revenue, traffic, or product performance
Output includes at least 3 distinct JTBD statements covering different user segments or use cases
Output does NOT contain generic placeholder text like [situation] or [motivation]

name: customer-journey-map-fintech
prompt: I'm working on a fintech app for small business invoicing. Can you map out the customer journey from awareness to advocacy? I want to identify where we're losing people.

expectations.description: Output includes all 6 journey stages: Awareness, Consideration, Decision, Purchase, Onboarding, Retention/Advocacy
Each stage includes pain points or friction points, since the user specifically asked about where they're losing people
Output includes a journey map table or structured format with columns for Actions, Thoughts, Emotions, Pain Points, and Opportunities
Content is specific to fintech/invoicing context, mentioning relevant concepts like payments, cash flow, accounting, or invoice management
Output identifies specific opportunities or recommendations to reduce drop-off at each stage

name: interview-framework-healthcare
prompt: We're launching a telehealth platform and need to interview potential users (both patients and providers). Can you give me an interview guide I can use for customer discovery?

expectations.description: Output includes structured interview sections with time allocations (Opening, Current State, Pain Points, Ideal State, Closing or similar)
Interview guide includes actual sample questions, not just topic headers
Guide addresses BOTH user segments mentioned: patients and healthcare providers with distinct questions or sections for each
Questions are open-ended (starting with how, what, tell me, walk me through) rather than yes/no
Output includes guidance on rapport building, recording permission, or follow-up protocol

name: segmentation-ambiguous-market
prompt: help me segment customers for a new product - it's like notion but for construction teams. not sure where to start honestly

expectations.description: Output uses multiple segmentation dimensions (demographic, behavioral, needs-based, or value-based)
Output includes a segment prioritization framework or table with criteria like size, need intensity, accessibility, and competition
Segments are specific to construction industry context (e.g., general contractors, subcontractors, project managers, site supervisors)
Despite the vague prompt, the skill provides actionable structure rather than asking excessive clarifying questions before producing output
Output identifies at least 3 distinct customer segments with descriptions

name: review-mining-competitor
prompt: I want to analyze what people are saying about our competitors on G2 and Capterra. We're in the CRM space competing with HubSpot and Pipedrive. Can you help me set up a review mining framework?

expectations.description: Output references specific review platforms mentioned by the user (G2, Capterra) and potentially others
Output includes categories for what to look for: repeated complaints, feature requests, competitor comparisons, use case descriptions, or emotional language
Framework is structured for CRM-specific analysis, mentioning relevant features like pipeline management, contact management, or integrations
Output provides a methodology or template for capturing and organizing review insights systematically
Output references both competitors mentioned (HubSpot and Pipedrive) in the analysis framework

name: full-research-summary-output
prompt: We're a DevOps monitoring startup. I need a complete customer research summary document that I can share with my co-founder and our seed investors. Cover personas, JTBD, journey insights, and pain points.

expectations.description: Output follows the Customer Research Summary structure with sections for Key Segments, Personas, Jobs-to-be-Done, Journey Insights, Pain Points, and Opportunities
Output includes Trend Indicators section with directional assessments (INC/DEC/CONST or equivalent)
Pain points are ranked/numbered rather than presented as an unordered list
Content is specific to DevOps monitoring domain, mentioning concepts like uptime, alerts, incident response, or infrastructure
Output is investor-ready: professional tone, no placeholder brackets remaining, and includes quantifiable or structured data
